name: Roast
roasterInfo: Roasts in-house on a Probat drum, rotating single-origins with a clean, classic Nordic-leaning profile.
description: An industrial-chic roastery in Scheepvaartkwartier, a benchmark for third-wave coffee in Rotterdam and one of the most photographed cafés in the city.

name: Cafecito Meent
description: A small, buzzing espresso bar on Meent — famous for its Cuban-sandwich lunches, sharp flat whites and a rotating bean list.

name: Schot Coffee Roasters
roasterInfo: Father-and-son micro-roastery dialling in light-to-medium roasts on a small Giesen, with a strong focus on seasonal African lots.
description: A tucked-away roastery-café in the Agniesebuurt — open, bright, and favoured by Rotterdam's coffee geeks for the rotating V60 menu.

name: Ripsnorter
description: A warm neighbourhood espresso bar on Nieuwe Binnenweg — changing guest roasters, a short natural-wine list, and the city's friendliest baristas.

name: unfiltered
roasterInfo: Works with European micro-roasters on a rotation; expect a lot from Friedhats, April and Manhattan Coffee Roasters.
description: A minimalist brew bar on Nieuwe Binnenweg — all white tile, Hario V60s and single-origin filters. Go for the slow-bar experience.

name: Espressobar Kopi Soesoe
description: A tiny, Indonesian-inflected espresso bar on the Kralingen side of the dike. Flat whites, homemade cookies, and a warm welcome.

name: Sound Kitchen
description: A music-loving all-day café and kitchen — strong brunch menu, Man Met Bril beans on espresso, and vinyl always on the turntable.

name: Harvest Cafe & Bakery
description: A bright café-bakery where the sourdough and cinnamon buns are as considered as the coffee. Family-run and almost always busy on weekends.

name: Hopper Noord
description: The Oude Noorden outpost of Hopper — a bright, high-ceilinged café that doubles as a local office for freelancers. Great lunches, solid espresso.

name: Man Met Bril Koffie
roasterInfo: Philip Reade's roastery, housed under the old railway viaduct in Agniesebuurt. One of the most respected roasters in the Netherlands.
description: Rotterdam's signature roaster — iconic viaduct location, friendly team, and beans that appear on menus across the country.

name: Man Met Bril Koffie — Hotel New York
roasterInfo: Serves Man Met Bril's rotating roasts on La Marzocco.
description: The Man Met Bril bar tucked inside Hotel New York on the Kop van Zuid — stunning water views and a full filter menu.

name: Giraffe Coffee Bar & Academy
description: A combined bar and training academy — the team here has coached multiple Dutch Barista Championship finalists. Ask about latte art classes.

name: Evermore
description: A plant-filled central café with a short, precise coffee menu and a loyal lunch crowd from the surrounding offices.

name: No 10 Coffee
description: A cosy espresso bar in Rotterdam's gallery district. Great cortados and enough window seats for an afternoon's reading.

name: 33rd Street Coffee
description: A New-York-styled neighbourhood café with bagels, drip brew and a friendly, unfussy approach to specialty coffee.

name: SOCOCO Specialty Coffee
roasterInfo: Part of the SOCOCO social-enterprise project. Roasts in small batches and trains baristas from the local community.
description: A harbourside filter bar in Delfshaven's Schiemond — serious about brewing, with weekly cuppings open to the public.

name: SOCOCO W70 — Social Coffeebar
roasterInfo: Serves SOCOCO Specialty Coffee from the Schiemond roastery.
description: The business-district sibling of SOCOCO — quick, consistent espresso for Weena commuters and a purposeful social mission.

name: Kuub Coffee
description: A minimalist, cube-shaped café (hence the name) in the Oude Noorden — short menu, polished espresso and a rotating pastry list.

name: Dune
description: Sand-toned interiors, Mediterranean pastries and thoughtful single-origin espresso — a calm spot on a noisy street.

name: Coffee District
roasterInfo: Rotates European micro-roasters — regular appearances from La Cabra, The Barn and April.
description: A long bar on Willemsparkweg in Oud-Zuid — the perfect pre-Vondelpark flat white. Excellent dial-ins and very friendly staff.

name: NU Koffie & Delicatessen
description: Part deli, part coffee bar — Italian-leaning pastries, fresh sandwiches and perfectly pulled espresso on Haarlemmerdijk.

name: Harmani Microroasterie
roasterInfo: Small-batch micro-roastery focused on traceable, single-farm lots with a Nordic light profile.
description: A West-side micro-roastery with a tight bar — if you care about origin paperwork, this is the room.

name: Haku Specialty Coffee
roasterInfo: Works closely with Japanese and Nordic roasters.
description: Japanese-inflected minimalist brew bar on Haarlemmerdijk — V60 experts, matcha soft-serve, and a cult following.

name: Uncommon
description: A bright De Pijp bar with a changing guest roaster every fortnight — always worth a detour for the curated bean menu.

name: Esthétique Café
description: Thoughtfully designed all-white café near Vondelpark. Pastel-perfect, but the pour-overs are serious business.

name: De Koffieschenkerij
description: A café attached to the Oude Kerk with a beautiful garden courtyard. A rare quiet pocket in the Red Light District.

name: Priesthood Coffee
roasterInfo: Rotates through European specialty roasters with emphasis on naturals and experimental processes.
description: Tiny, obsessively detailed espresso bar on Stadhouderskade. Every shot is dialled in, every pour is weighed.

name: Five Ways Coffee Roasters — Boerhaaveplein
roasterInfo: Roasts on-site — a core Colombian for milk drinks and two rotating filter lots.
description: Five Ways' roastery location on Boerhaaveplein in Amsterdam-Oost — roomy, plant-filled, always smells of fresh batches.

name: Nomos Coffee
description: A quiet, precise little bar in the south of De Pijp — regular guest roasts and an impeccable flat white.

name: Caffènation Amsterdam
roasterInfo: The Amsterdam flagship of the long-running Antwerp roaster Caffènation — house blends plus seasonal single origins.
description: A spacious West-end bar from one of the Low Countries' most established specialty roasters.

name: LERA
description: Serious bar-focused specialty on Haarlemmerstraat — lots of natural wine, small plates at night, and rigorous espresso all day.

name: Taal Coffee
description: A calm Staatsliedenbuurt neighbourhood café — excellent brunch, strong pour-overs, and a soundtrack you'll want to Shazam.

name: Five Ways Coffee — Kinkerstraat
roasterInfo: Serves Five Ways' own roast from Boerhaaveplein.
description: The West outpost of Five Ways — compact, energetic, and where many Kinkerbuurt regulars start their day.

name: Rum Baba
description: A warm, wood-filled East-side café known for its rum baba cake, hearty brunch menu, and approachable espresso.

name: LOT61
roasterInfo: One of Amsterdam's biggest independent roasters, founded by Australians Marcus & Adam and now distributing nationwide.
description: The Kinkerstraat café of LOT61 — wide windows, concrete floors, and house roasts you'll recognise from menus all over the country.

name: Bocca Coffee
roasterInfo: A Dutch specialty pioneer since 2001 — direct-trade relationships, a roastery in Amsterdam, and a long-running partnership with Amsterdam Coffee Festival.
description: Bocca's Canal-Ring flagship on Kerkstraat — the bar where many Amsterdammers first fell in love with specialty coffee.

name: Scandinavian Embassy
roasterInfo: Serves exclusively Scandinavian roasters — expect La Cabra, Koppi, Drop Coffee and April.
description: An Amsterdam institution — Nordic-leaning coffees, reindeer-meat sandwiches, cardamom buns and a sun-trap of a terrace on Sarphatipark.

name: White Label Coffee West
roasterInfo: Francesco and Elmer's roastery — light roasts with a focus on transparent, long-term farm relationships.
description: A Bos en Lommer roastery-bar with a calm, lived-in feel. Many consider their filters the best in the west of the city.

name: Back to Black — Weteringstraat
roasterInfo: Rotates European roasters — frequent appearances from Manhattan Coffee Roasters and The Barn.
description: The original Back to Black — a sliver of a bar near Rijksmuseum, famous for its cookies and meticulous espresso.

name: Back to Black — Van Hallstraat
roasterInfo: Same curation as Weteringstraat — guest roaster rotations.
description: Back to Black's West location — roomier than the original, with the same legendary cookies and precise bar programme.

name: Screaming Beans
roasterInfo: One of Amsterdam's early specialty pioneers, still roasting and serving seasonal blends.
description: The Amstelstraat location of Screaming Beans — a steady, reliable central bar for a well-pulled cortado before dinner.

name: Espressofabriek Westergas
roasterInfo: Roasts its own blends and single-origins in small batches — a steady hand at medium profiles.
description: Espressofabriek's mothership in Westergasfabriek — a converted industrial space surrounded by Westerpark's greenery.

name: Espressofabriek IJburg
roasterInfo: Serves Espressofabriek's own roasts.
description: The IJburg outpost of Espressofabriek — bigger, brighter, family-friendly. A great first stop after a ferry ride east.

name: Espressofabriek Slibvijzel
roasterInfo: Serves Espressofabriek's own roasts.
description: A small Espressofabriek bar on a tucked-away canal. Quick, friendly, and reliably good espresso in the heart of town.

name: TOKI
description: A Japanese-Scandinavian minimalist café in the Haarlemmerbuurt — great matcha, top-tier pour-overs, and the calmest vibe in town.

name: Black Gold Amsterdam
description: Vinyl-lined espresso bar near Nieuwmarkt — records for sale, meticulously brewed coffee, and the best soundtrack in town.

name: Coffee Bru
roasterInfo: Works with East African cooperatives and rotates Rwandan and Burundian lots throughout the year.
description: A sunny Oost café on Beukenplein with roots in Rwanda — fantastic terrace, strong pour-overs, and a warm community feel.

name: Koffie & Ik
description: A friendly central Utrecht bar — tight menu, cheerful service, and a loyal breakfast-and-laptop crowd.

name: Dagger Coffee
roasterInfo: Roasts in small batches on a Giesen — light, clean profiles with serious attention to sourcing.
description: A small, design-led roastery-bar tucked away in Zijdebalen. Rotating filter menu and excellent cuppings by appointment.

name: Fufu Coffee Studio
description: A tiny, precise studio-style bar on Twijnstraat — just a few stools and very good coffee.

name: Kaffeetaria
description: A canal-side café-tavern on the Oudegracht — wharf-level terrace, excellent espresso, natural wine after 4 PM.

name: Koffie Leute
description: A compact, unhurried bar on Voorstraat — excellent flat whites and friendly regulars, perfect for a reading break.

name: Keen Coffee Bar
roasterInfo: One of Utrecht's headline roasters — supplies cafés across the Netherlands, with a focus on seasonality and traceability.
description: Keen's proeflokaal near Vredenburg — wide bar, full filter menu, and the city's go-to place to discover new origins.

name: 't Koffieboontje
description: A classic bean shop and café on Ganzenmarkt — wide retail selection and a short espresso menu for in-store tastings.

name: Ohøj Coffee Roasting
roasterInfo: Scandinavian-influenced light-roast roastery in the Cartesius district — tight focus on under-represented origins.
description: A roastery-workshop with an open bar — low-key, friendly, and a favourite stop for coffee geeks making the pilgrimage.

name: The Village
description: A warm, wood-panelled all-day café on Voorstraat — great for long meetings, cheese toasties and afternoon filter refills.

name: Rocking Chair
description: A snug espresso bar just off the Domtoren — eclectic interiors, precise shots, and a menu that changes with the roaster's lots.

name: Cupp
description: A popular Nobelstraat café with great cakes and consistently well-dialled espresso. A favourite among students.

name: 30ML Nachtegaalstraat
description: A precise, specialty-only espresso bar — 30 millilitres, minimum fuss, maximum attention to the shot.

name: Ukiyo Koffie
address: Twijnstraat aan de Werf 46, 3511 ZM Utrecht
roasterInfo: Rotates Japanese and European light-roast specialists.
description: A wharf-level Japanese-styled brew bar on Twijnstraat — ceramic cups, V60 purism, and some of the city's cleanest filters.

name: Mos Coffee Bar
description: A laid-back Biltstraat bar — plant-filled, pet-friendly, and consistently well-dialled espresso.
